name: Blocker-Mover Offense
description: A highly structured motion offense that divides the five players into two distinct roles: "Blockers" (typically forwards/centers) who constantly set screens, and "Movers" (typically guards/wings) who use these screens to cut, curl, or spot-up for open shots. It relies heavily on pin-downs, flare screens, and cross screens, demanding high stamina and screen-reading abilities.

playtypes.name: Cut
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player cuts without a screen (including UCLA, flex, etc.).

playtypes.name: Off-screen
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player uses an off-ball screen.

playtypes.name: Spot-up
playtypes.description: Possessions where the player stands still or moves without using an off-ball screen.
